Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.4/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/4f11de0c-e94b-11e2-bc4d-52540035b04c).  Ran 17 tests.  2 tests failed: sanity-hsm, conf-sanity.

Patch Set 3:

The test uses the variable $HT to find lhsmtool_posix executable. The path in the rpm is /usr/sbin/lhsmtool_posix, I do not understand why HT is set to the wrong value (which is also not the default so something must set it). Is there a special way to init this variable?

Patch Set 3:

Got it, in sanity-hsm, export HT=${HT:-"$LUSTRE/utils/lhsmtool_posix"} so this mean LUSTRE is set to /usr/lib4/.
I will find a better way to set HT default
Uploaded patch set 4.

Patch Set 4:

/usr/lib64/lustre/tests/sanity-hsm.sh: line 97: lhsmtool_posix: command not found

Patch Set 4:

I do not understand what happen. After a make rpm, i have the lhsmtool_posix binary but in jenkins log I do not find the compilation nor the installation so it is why the test failed. 

Also in other patches (like LU-2062) I have sanity-hsm (smaller) with the same makefile and it works. Any idea to solve this is welcome

Patch Set 4:

Hi JC,

I just saw that the lhsmtool_posix copytool was added in patch http://review.whamcloud.com/4737 . So, if this patch did not finally depend on that patch, the copytool could not be built into the packages for this patch.

Patch Set 4:

I confirm Jian comment, your branch only contains change #4736 for LU-2062, when you require #4737 too to get lhsm_posix stuff.

Patch Set 4:

When you rebase, wlease run contrib/scripts/checkpatch.pl on this before resubmitting as there are several lines over 80 chars.

Patch Set 4:

good catch, thank you all. In fact to be successful this patch needs all other HSM patches. The issue is that the full stack is made of 3 independent stacks to help the review and landing (each stack is made of up to 4 patches). If I make a single stack for this patch, the dependencies and review, maloo will break regulary. So let's wait for few landing of others and I will rebase this patch.

About the 80 character issue, it mainly comes from string messages in shell which are not easy to break in multiple lines. I will see how I can improve this.

Even if maloo failed, please review the script.

Patch Set 4:

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.4/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/5c238664-efda-11e2-b957-52540035b04c).  Ran 17 tests.  2 tests failed: sanity-hsm, conf-sanity.

Patch Set 4: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(13 inline comments)

More review comments will be added.

Patch Set 4: (50 inline comments)

Please refer to https://wiki.hpdd.intel.com/display/PUB/Test+Coding+Style for Lustre test script coding style. Thanks.

Patch Set 4:

Hello JC,

While looking into sanity-hsm.sh, I found that in copytool_setup() only one copytool was started and the host of it (aka agent) was combined with the local Lustre client node.

In LU-2064, the document of HSM-16.lyx described that we need introduce AGTCOUNT, AGTDEV{n}, SINGLEAGT, agt{n} and maybe agt{n}_HOST, etc. to support multiple remote agents. I was assigned the task to add those stuff and more test cases into sanity-hsm.sh to validate multiple copytools serving different archive back-ends or the same archive back-end under $SHARED_DIRECTORY (which is visible by all the agents). 

I'm still learning and have a basic question now:

In a test cluster which has multiple Lustre client nodes, if I started copytools on remote clients (not on the local client), will the "lfs hsm_*" operations on the local client work correctly? In other words, must "lfs hsm_*" operations be run on a node which has copytool started?

Thanks.

Patch Set 4:

"lfs hsm_*" operations must be run on any standard client which does not have a CT running on it. The external interactions with CT are start/stop/may be search. All others interactions with CT are done internally to Lustre.

Patch Set 4:

I will work on the review comments during the week

Patch Set 4: (57 inline comments)

In all the script:
- I will replace use of `` by $()
- I will use $tfile in all the tests
- will avoid \ at end of line

So you will have a runnable version early next week.

parents.subject: LU-1346 libcfs: cleanup macros in kp30.h

commit.subject: LU-3561 tests: HSM sanity test suite
commit.message:
LU-3561 tests: HSM sanity test suite

Add sanity-hsm.sh. It is a suite of tests used to validate
HSM features.

Signed-off-by: JC Lafoucriere <jacques-charles.lafoucriere@cea.fr>
Signed-off-by: Aurelien Degremont <aurelien.degremont@cea.fr>
Signed-off-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Signed-off-by: Jian Yu <jian.yu@intel.com>
Change-Id: I0b88d68806158741e35ac16b124e7aff54581b40
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/6913
Tested-by: Hudson
Tested-by: Maloo <whamcloud.maloo@gmail.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
